source.title: Theme Park setup and defend guide (all four sites) - Littleton
source.notes: Auto-generated YouTube captions (creator refers to himself as 'Littleton'/'Litton'). Ranked-focused generic setups, not pro-league lineups. No corresponding Attack-side transcript was available for this map, so this file is Defense-only. Room/callout names are the creator's informal callouts, not verified wiki callouts. Two operator mentions in the Bunk/Daycare and Initiation/Office sections were ambiguous in the ASR text and are flagged in-line rather than guessed.

reinforcements.location: Back Throne wall into Maintenance (x2)
reinforcements.note: Part of the standard 7-reinforcement setup for this site.

reinforcements.location: Back Throne wall into Barrels (x2)
reinforcements.note: Part of the standard 7-reinforcement setup for this site.

reinforcements.location: Armory wall onto bottom yellow stairs area (x2)
reinforcements.note: Part of the standard 7-reinforcement setup for this site.

reinforcements.location: Split hatch
reinforcements.note: The 7th and final reinforcement. 'Split' is the area just inside the site where you can go left or right.

headHoles.location: Feet holes along the wall near the anchor point, HALF the wall only
headHoles.note: Only feet-hole roughly halfway along this wall, stopping before the far end. Lets you see ankles and shoot heads on anyone walking in from a strong, safe anchor spot (this side of the site is soft; the Throne/back side is fully hard). Attackers peeking from prone here only see a pillar.

headHoles.location: Same wall, full length (DO NOT do this)
headHoles.note: Common mistake: feet-holing the entire wall gains nothing extra for the defender (you already see ankles/shoot heads with the half-length version) but gives attackers a long prone sightline all the way back into Back Furnace, which punishes whoever is playing near Furnace. Stop at roughly the halfway point.

headHoles.location: C4 hole above the yellow wall (near default plant), first spot
headHoles.note: If the yellow wall opens, the default plant is tucked behind a pillar (or the boxes) that's otherwise nearly impossible to stop. This hole lets you throw C4 over onto the plant spot to deny it.

headHoles.location: C4 hole above the yellow wall, second spot
headHoles.note: Paired with the first C4 hole for the same default-plant denial purpose.

headHoles.location: Holes above each reinforced wall, for impact-tricking thermite
headHoles.note: Cut a small hole above each wall so you can throw an impact grenade onto a thermite charge placed on the far side (splash damage destroys the thermite charge). Works the same for Kaid's or Ace's charges. With Ace charges you can throw the impact from much further away; thermite has to be placed low on the wall or the impact won't reach it, so listen for the placement sound before throwing.

headHoles.location: Head hole overlooking the door from Gong
headHoles.note: Strong late-round kill angle — attackers running from Gong almost never check this angle. Best played in the first 20-30 seconds of the round while waiting for an early rusher.

headHoles.location: Feet holes at the long corner near Split, facing Drug/Lab corridor
headHoles.note: Sometimes worth making. Don't go prone behind them (prone exposes you to headshots) — hold the angle standing/crouched so you see feet first. Covers anyone coming through Drug into Lab and eventually toward Split door.

hatches.note: Reinforced as the 7th reinforcement (see reinforcements list).

operatorRecommendations.note: You need at least one, ideally two players upstairs — attackers can reach this area within about 30 seconds if it's left completely open, and the balcony gives a strong angle onto Dragon late in the round.

generalTips.note: Considered by the creator the strongest and easiest site to defend on the map, and the hardest for attackers to take — site setup itself is simple.
The anchor-point side of the site is soft, but everything on the Throne side (the far side) is hard/non-soft.

Only about half of the floor near Barrels/Throne is C4-able — the wood-panel flooring marks where it becomes cable-able (i.e., breachable from below); the rest, including all of Daycare's floor above, is soft from below too.
There are banners around the site useful for aim warm-up before the round.

reinforcements.location: Bunk wall into Initiation (x2)
reinforcements.note: Part of the 8-reinforcement setup for this site.

reinforcements.location: Left-hand side of Bunk wall into Break Room
reinforcements.note: Part of the 8-reinforcement setup.

reinforcements.location: Right-hand side of the Waiting Room double wall, through to Office
reinforcements.note: It's a double wall but only the right-hand side is reinforced; the other side is left soft.

reinforcements.location: Two Daycare walls at the bottom, into Cafe
reinforcements.note: Both the left and right-hand sides reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Two walls from Office/Daycare area into top yellow stairs
reinforcements.note: Both reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Wall from Bunk area into the room that lets attackers cut off most of Bunks (left soft = bad)
reinforcements.note: If left soft, attackers can open a line of sight there that cuts off most of Bunks. Recommended to reinforce it — costs a reinforcement but denies that angle entirely (they can still hard-breach if they choose).

reinforcements.location: Soft wall from Cafe area into Vault
reinforcements.note: Recommended reinforce. Without it, attackers can soft-breach and hold a long angle down the corridor from a bookshelf spot, or cut around into site.

reinforcements.location: Cafe wall near the Cafe-side corridor
reinforcements.note: Reinforced for the same reason as the Vault-side wall — denies the long corridor angle.

headHoles.location: Feet holes on either side of the wall near Cafe
headHoles.note: Cafe double and Waiting double are the two main push points for this site. These feet holes let a defender playing behind the bomb shoot ankles as attackers exit Cafe in either direction, instead of letting them walk out freely if all four walls at the bottom were reinforced.

headHoles.location: Line of sight near Initiation extension (if extending there)
headHoles.note: Only relevant if choosing the extend-to-Initiation option — cut a line of sight to help hold the hatch. Creator prefers NOT to extend to Initiation.

headHoles.location: Ratty punch hole low in the wall, near where the other hole is
headHoles.note: Go prone and make a very low, hard-to-spot punch hole here — lets you hold the yellow stairs in relative safety. Attackers dropping the security hatch and looking this way notice the bigger hole in the wall first and get killed from the punch hole before spotting it.

rotates.location: Back of Break Room, toward Upper Arcade
rotates.note: Essential rotate. If you're in Break Room and attackers repel the nearby windows, this door is your only other way out. Also gives an angle toward Waiting Room, letting you fight from a different position if pushed instead of being stuck with one exposed exit.

rotates.location: Rotate hole in Daycare (exact spot flexible, avoid the window)
rotates.note: Position doesn't matter much except don't put it in line with the nearby window, or attackers can pick off anyone using the rotate from there. Can be placed more to the left if preferred.

Optional extension: castle the door off, make a line of sight, reinforce a wall, and make a line of sight to hold the hatch. Creator isn't a fan of this option and prefers a simpler reinforce-and-fall-back plan instead.

hatches.note: Always open (not reinforced) — lets you hold down into the Barrels window, a common rush point via bottom Arcade window or around from Barrels. Also allows dropping C4 later in the round if pushed.

generalTips.note: Creator's second-favourite site on the map (behind Throne Room), ahead of Initiation/Office in his ranking, though he says that's personal preference.

Someone needs to hold the far side of the map near Waiting Room — start in Cash, kill a couple of drones coming through the Cash door or Dragon, maybe get drones off the security hatch, then fall back into site rather than holding that area long-term.

Preferred plan for the off-site area: reinforce, play a slow anchor there (e.g. with a Ram-style operator) to get intel/kills off a couple of drones, force attackers to burn flashbangs or a fear charge, then fall back — watching out for Office and Cash door on the way.

Late round, attackers who've run out of ideas from Waiting Room will bait the hole-in-the-wall angle near the punch hole and wait for defenders to run out toward it — a dangerous open area with peeks from everywhere.

reinforcements.location: Two walls from Office out into the top yellow area
reinforcements.note: Part of the 10-reinforcement setup for this site.

reinforcements.location: Left-hand side Office wall out into Waiting
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Left-hand side Office wall out into Cash, toward Initiation
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Left-hand side wall from Initiation out into Security
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Two walls from Initiation out into Bunks
reinforcements.note: Both reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Two walls in Cafe (above this site)
reinforcements.note: Reinforced for the same reason as on the Bunk/Daycare site — denies the long corridor angle and stops attackers walking straight across the gangway into Vault/site.

reinforcements.location: Cafe wall (the one kept soft elsewhere, reinforced here to stop the gangway walk-across)
reinforcements.note: If this is left soft, attackers can soft-breach it and walk straight across the gangway into Vault and into site — reinforce it. They rarely bother opening it since it's a loud soft breach, but don't risk it.

reinforcements.location: Wall next to the reinforced wall near the repel/thermite-bait spot
reinforcements.note: Deliberately opened (not left soft, not reinforced) as a preventative measure — see general tips for the thermite-blast-through trick this counters.

headHoles.location: Head hole(s) from Office into Cash
headHoles.note: Lets you peek from here and hold Dragon. If both nearby walls are reinforced, attackers only need to worry about one peek angle from behind the boxes — this head hole forces them to respect a second angle even if you rarely use it.

headHoles.location: Head hole on the Security wall
headHoles.note: Considered more important than the Office/Cash one — Security is one of the most popular ways in via the hatch, plus the window and the door to top Dragon. Lets you peek from an unpredictable side instead of only holding the door, which a single well-placed Kali/Ela-style flush grenade through the door can otherwise clear entirely.

headHoles.location: Line of sight down toward back of Cash
headHoles.note: This is the creator's normal playing spot on this site (with an ACOG-class weapon) — lets you hold Waiting Room double door, adding one more angle (on top of the door, rotate, and Dragon long angle) attackers already have to worry about there. Source audio named the operator here phonetically as 'one I' and, in an alternate mention, 'Maloy' — neither matches a real Defense operator with confidence; treat the operator ID as unconfirmed, but the ACOG line-of-sight placement itself is clearly described.

headHoles.location: Hole plus a low ratty punch hole in the same wall near Break Room (mirrors the Bunk/Daycare trick)
headHoles.note: Same trick as used on the Bunk/Daycare site: cut a visible hole plus a much harder-to-spot low punch hole. Lets you hold Security door and Top Dragon — attackers dropping the security hatch notice the big hole first and are dead from the punch hole before spotting it.

rotates.note: Lets you get out of Office and attack the Waiting door from a different angle. Without it, the only way out of Office is through the one door attackers can hold from the double door in Waiting — this rotate forces them to worry about more than one angle.

rotates.location: Small rotate at the back of the site, near the shrine
rotates.note: Gives access into Initiation if attackers are holding behind the shrine at the back of the site — without it, the only way in is through the one door they're already watching.

rotates.location: Rotate near Break Room (same as Bunk/Daycare site)
rotates.note: Same setup as the Bunk/Daycare rotate: run out, peek Upper Arcade, come back in, peek Waiting if needed.

rotates.location: Additional rotate near the same Break Room area
rotates.note: Also usable to hold the stairs and to hold anyone coming out of Cafe from the other side, instead of only worrying about the Waiting double door.

gadgetPlacements.location: Wall next to the main reinforcement, deliberately opened
gadgetPlacements.note: If a thermite charge is placed right up against the solid reinforced wall next to it, the explosion blows a hole through the reinforcement too. Pre-emptively destroying this soft wall yourself denies attackers that trick for almost no cost — you can then Bandit or Kaid (source audio said 'Cade') the reinforced wall to stop thermite entirely.

operatorRecommendations.note: Electrify the reinforced wall next to the deliberately-opened soft wall so thermite placed there can't take it even via the blast-through trick.

generalTips.note: Creator's favourite of the four sites alongside Bunk/Daycare, but the most setup-intensive — around 10 reinforcements and 5 rotates plus several lines of sight, so the whole team needs to know the plan.

When deciding whether to cut a head hole, also consider what the attacker gains from the opposite side — several of these head holes give attackers nothing useful to look at, so there's little downside to making them.

Someone needs to hold the far side of the map (Waiting/Cafe/Security) just like on Bunk/Daycare — if nobody holds that side, attackers can walk freely from Cafe to a single door, thermite one wall and Ace-charge the other, and be in.

reinforcements.location: Two walls into Barrels, from the corridor outside Lab double
reinforcements.note: Considered absolutely imperative reinforcements. Part of the 6-reinforcement setup.

reinforcements.location: Right-hand side wall looking out from Lab into the corridor
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Three walls on the left-hand side of Lab out to the corridor
reinforcements.note: Only the right-hand side of this section is left soft.

reinforcements.location: No reinforcements around the toilet/bathroom area
reinforcements.note: No soft walls there worth reinforcing.

headHoles.location: Rotate/peek hole between sites, tight to the wall
headHoles.note: Needs to be cut really tight to the wall to work. Lets you peek onto the window from here without directly holding the window itself.

headHoles.location: Feet holes, same style as Throne Room (kept low, not head-height)
headHoles.note: Make sure these are genuinely feet holes, not waist-height — aim the shotgun closer to the floor than feels natural. Play from behind the bomb; anyone pushing from Split/Throne or down the corridor gets shot in the feet as they come out.

headHoles.location: Feet holes near the bathroom approach
headHoles.note: Covers anyone walking through from the bathroom direction — attackers coming this way won't be looking at these holes, giving a free angle. Don't overdo the number of feet holes here since attackers can also hold the area from the window.

rotates.location: Rotate between sites (same hole used for the window peek)
rotates.note: Doesn't need to be used for peeking the window if you'd rather not — every good player will camp that window baiting peeks — but the hole itself also gives another route back into the site, improving maneuverability. Attackers get nothing from this angle; the geometry outside is too tight for them to hold it.

gadgetPlacements.note: If this wall/window area opens up, you can stand there to hold the window, and launch C4 through it at anyone jumping in.

gadgetPlacements.location: Any wall where the top can be shotgunned open (applies map-wide)
gadgetPlacements.note: If a hard breach starts on such a wall, impact-trick the ceiling/top above it the same way as on Throne Room — destroys the hard breach charge on the other side.
One of the best anchor points on this site — no soft wall here, so it's safe to hold.

Good spot to place a shield — you're pinned into a corner but very secure. The Cafe balcony soft wall above doesn't start until further along, so this exact spot is safe from above. Vulnerable to grenades thrown from the window, which force you toward the window or further into the room.
gadgetPlacements.operator: Clash (not specified by name in source — Clash is the only Defense-side shield operator, so this is the confident inference)

gadgetPlacements.note: Attackers often jump straight through this window — place a proximity/notification-style gadget (source audio suggested 'goo mine' [Lesion] or an 'Al mine' which is unclear/possibly Alibi's Prisma gadget) here as an early warning. Treat the specific operator as unconfirmed; the placement concept (early-warning gadget covering the window jump-in) is clear.

operatorRecommendations.note: Source audio referenced a 'goo mine' near the window jump-in spot, consistent with Lesion's Gu mines, but no operator name was stated outright — treat as unconfirmed.

generalTips.note: Creator's least favourite of the four sites, though not 'horrendous' — the other three are simply considered stronger.
This site used to be popular in pro league when Theme Park was in the competitive map pool; it isn't anymore, and what works in competitive doesn't necessarily translate to ranked.
Site is tough to hold solo-mentality — needs all five players understanding their job, since there are many easy entry points (window, back door, etc.).
Must reinforce the double doors here — if left soft, attackers can open them and see straight through the whole site due to how open/expansive it is.
Watch out for the hatch above in case attackers drop in from there while you're holding this site.
